title: Care for a Mild Illness
summary: Take care of yourself at home when you feel unwell by resting, staying hydrated, and using over-the-counter remedies safely.
learnerPromise: Take care of yourself when you feel unwell.
whyItMatters: Everyone feels sick sometimes. Knowing how to rest, hydrate, and use basic remedies helps you recover faster and feel more comfortable.
realLifeUses: Resting at home with a cold or mild flu
Managing a headache with rest and hydration
Knowing what medicine to take for a mild fever
Deciding when to stay home instead of going out
youWillLearn: How to read an over-the-counter medicine label
Basic steps for resting and recovering at home
How to stay hydrated when you are sick
Warning signs that mean you should see a doctor
miniChallenge: Check your medicine cabinet for one item you are unsure about and read its label.
steps: Find a comfortable place to rest with easy access to water and tissues.
Drink water, herbal tea, or broth regularly to stay hydrated.
Check your medicine cabinet for remedies that match your symptoms.
Read the label carefully for dosage, timing, and warnings.
Monitor how you feel and contact a doctor if symptoms get worse or last more than a few days.
completionCriteria: You found a comfortable setup for resting
You read a medicine label and understood the dosage
You know at least one warning sign that means you should call a doctor
commonProblems: You are not sure which medicine to take — ask a pharmacist or read labels carefully
You feel too sick to prepare food or drinks — ask someone nearby to help
Symptoms are not improving after several days — contact a medical professional
tips: Sleep is one of the best ways to help your body recover
Warm liquids like soup or tea can soothe a sore throat and help with hydration
Do not take more medicine than the label recommends, even if you feel very unwell
helpBoundary: Ask a medical professional if symptoms worsen or do not improve within a few days.
